title: lithocarpus sulitii
intro: Lithocarpus sulitii, also known as the Sulit's tan oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. It is a type of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and improving digestion. Lithocarpus sulitii is often used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations include extracts and infusions that can be taken orally or applied topically.
